We are recruiting to fill the position below:

  "title": "Technical Project Manager (Fintech Platforms & Digital Financial Systems)",
  "description": "At Strivo Labs, we are passionate about helping businesses scale with speed and precision. We specialise in providing end-to-end digital solutions, from custom design and MVP development to automation and content creation.

Our work includes building customer-facing platforms, financial APIs, embedded finance systems, digital lending solutions, and enterprise automation tools. As our client portfolio continues to grow, we are seeking a candidate&nbsp;to lead the delivery of complex digital products and financial technology platforms for our clients in the capacity below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Technical Project Manager (Fintech Platforms &amp; Digital Financial Systems)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Mode: Hybrid

Role Overview


	As our client portfolio continues to grow, we are seeking a Technical Project Manager to lead the successful delivery of complex digital and financial technology platforms for our clients.
	The Technical Project Manager will be responsible for overseeing the end-to-end delivery of fintech and financial infrastructure projects executed by Strivolabs.
	This role ensures that projects are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with client expectations, while coordinating closely with engineering teams, architects, and external stakeholders.
	The Technical Project Manager will act as the central coordination point between clients and internal delivery teams, ensuring that requirements are clearly defined, development progress is tracked, and project milestones are successfully achieved.


Key Responsibilities
Project Leadership &amp; Delivery:


	Manage the end-to-end lifecycle of fintech platform and digital financial system projects.
	Develop project plans, timelines, and delivery milestones.
	Ensure projects are delivered within scope, budget, and timelines.
	Monitor development progress and proactively manage risks and dependencies.
	Coordinate cross-functional teams to ensure smooth project execution.


Requirements &amp; Project Planning:


	Gather and document project requirements from clients and stakeholders.
	Translate business needs into clear technical and functional specifications.
	Work with product and engineering teams to define deliverables and implementation plans.
	Maintain project documentation including scope, timelines, and deliverables.


Technical Coordination:


	Work closely with engineering teams to ensure proper implementation of project requirements.
	Support discussions around APIs, integrations, and system architecture.
	Ensure solutions are designed for security, scalability, and reliability.
	Coordinate system integrations across fintech platforms, APIs, and financial services infrastructure.


Stakeholder Management:


	Act as the primary point of contact between client teams and internal engineering teams.
	Facilitate project meetings, status updates, and milestone reviews.
	Manage expectations and ensure clear communication across all stakeholders.
	Provide regular reports on project progress, risks, and delivery timelines.


Implementation &amp; Deployment:


	Coordinate testing, staging, and production deployments.
	Ensure solutions meet functional, performance, and security requirements.
	Support pilot launches and production rollouts.
	Ensure smooth transition from development to operational deployment.


Required Skills &amp; Experience


	5+ years of experience in Technical Project Management or Technology Delivery roles.
	Experience delivering fintech, banking, or financial technology platforms.
	Experience managing complex software projects involving multiple stakeholders.
	Experience coordinating engineering teams on platform-based or API-driven systems.


Technical Understanding:
Candidates should have working knowledge of:


	APIs and system integrations
	software architecture concepts
	cloud infrastructure environments
	financial systems and digital platforms
	Coding is not required, but candidates must be comfortable working closely with engineers and discussing technical implementation.


Project Management Skills:


	Agile or hybrid project management methodologies
	sprint planning and delivery coordination
	Risk and dependency management
	project documentation and reporting
	stakeholder communication and expectation management


Preferred Experience:
Experience working with:


	fintech companies or financial institutions
	digital lending or payments systems
	core banking integrations
	Financial APIs and platform ecosystems will be highly advantageous.


What Success Looks Like in This Role:
Within the first six months, the Technical Project Manager will:


	Successfully deliver key fintech platform projects for Strivolabs clients.
	Establish strong collaboration between engineering teams and client stakeholders.
	Ensure projects are delivered on schedule and aligned with business requirements.
	Improve project visibility, documentation, and delivery processes across the organisation.


The role works closely with:


	Engineering teams and technical architects
	Financial services clients
	Implementation and integration partners
	Internal product and delivery teams to deliver secure, scalable financial technology platforms.

Technical Project Manager (Fintech Platforms & Digital Financial Systems) Job at Strivo Labs




        
    

        
        At Strivo Labs, we are passionate about helping businesses scale with speed and precision. We specialise in providing end-to-end digital solutions, from custom design and MVP development to automation and content creation.

Our work includes building customer-facing platforms, financial APIs, embedded finance systems, digital lending solutions, and enterprise automation tools. As our client portfolio continues to grow, we are seeking a candidate to lead the delivery of complex digital products and financial technology platforms for our clients in the capacity below:

Job Position: Technical Project Manager (Fintech Platforms & Digital Financial Systems)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Mode: Hybrid

Role Overview


	As our client portfolio continues to grow, we are seeking a Technical Project Manager to lead the successful delivery of complex digital and financial technology platforms for our clients.
	The Technical Project Manager will be responsible for overseeing the end-to-end delivery of fintech and financial infrastructure projects executed by Strivolabs.
	This role ensures that projects are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with client expectations, while coordinating closely with engineering teams, architects, and external stakeholders.
	The Technical Project Manager will act as the central coordination point between clients and internal delivery teams, ensuring that requirements are clearly defined, development progress is tracked, and project milestones are successfully achieved.


Key Responsibilities
Project Leadership & Delivery:


	Manage the end-to-end lifecycle of fintech platform and digital financial system projects.
	Develop project plans, timelines, and delivery milestones.
	Ensure projects are delivered within scope, budget, and timelines.
	Monitor development progress and proactively manage risks and dependencies.
	Coordinate cross-functional teams to ensure smooth project execution.


Requirements & Project Planning:


	Gather and document project requirements from clients and stakeholders.
	Translate business needs into clear technical and functional specifications.
	Work with product and engineering teams to define deliverables and implementation plans.
	Maintain project documentation including scope, timelines, and deliverables.


Technical Coordination:


	Work closely with engineering teams to ensure proper implementation of project requirements.
	Support discussions around APIs, integrations, and system architecture.
	Ensure solutions are designed for security, scalability, and reliability.
	Coordinate system integrations across fintech platforms, APIs, and financial services infrastructure.


Stakeholder Management:


	Act as the primary point of contact between client teams and internal engineering teams.
	Facilitate project meetings, status updates, and milestone reviews.
	Manage expectations and ensure clear communication across all stakeholders.
	Provide regular reports on project progress, risks, and delivery timelines.


Implementation & Deployment:


	Coordinate testing, staging, and production deployments.
	Ensure solutions meet functional, performance, and security requirements.
	Support pilot launches and production rollouts.
	Ensure smooth transition from development to operational deployment.


Required Skills & Experience


	5+ years of experience in Technical Project Management or Technology Delivery roles.
	Experience delivering fintech, banking, or financial technology platforms.
	Experience managing complex software projects involving multiple stakeholders.
	Experience coordinating engineering teams on platform-based or API-driven systems.


Technical Understanding:
Candidates should have working knowledge of:


	APIs and system integrations
	software architecture concepts
	cloud infrastructure environments
	financial systems and digital platforms
	Coding is not required, but candidates must be comfortable working closely with engineers and discussing technical implementation.


Project Management Skills:


	Agile or hybrid project management methodologies
	sprint planning and delivery coordination
	Risk and dependency management
	project documentation and reporting
	stakeholder communication and expectation management


Preferred Experience:
Experience working with:


	fintech companies or financial institutions
	digital lending or payments systems
	core banking integrations
	Financial APIs and platform ecosystems will be highly advantageous.


What Success Looks Like in This Role:
Within the first six months, the Technical Project Manager will:


	Successfully deliver key fintech platform projects for Strivolabs clients.
	Establish strong collaboration between engineering teams and client stakeholders.
	Ensure projects are delivered on schedule and aligned with business requirements.
	Improve project visibility, documentation, and delivery processes across the organisation.


The role works closely with:


	Engineering teams and technical architects
	Financial services clients
	Implementation and integration partners
	Internal product and delivery teams to deliver secure, scalable financial technology platforms.

Quality Assurance Engineer, Software Development Job at NewGlobe




        
    

        
        NewGlobe supports visionary governments to transform public education systems, the cornerstone of a prosperous, equitable, and peaceful society. With a comprehensive system transformation platform and data-driven educational services, NewGlobe delivers rapid and dramatic improvements in learning outcomes at state and nationwide scale. Through building impactful partnerships and programmes, NewGlobe ensures that all children have access to an education that will develop their full potential and create a foundation for growth and prosperity.

NewGlobe leverages more than a decade of educational experience and proven impact in integrated school management, teacher professional development, instructional design innovation, technological system support, child-centered classroom practice, and parent engagement -  all grounded in learning science - to ensure each teacher is empowered to engage children in transformational learning, and all children have the opportunity to develop to their full potential. Every day, NewGlobe supports governments to solve what were once seen as intractable problems, and by doing so, ensures this generation will be able to grow up to lead more fulfilled lives, while simultaneously building economic prosperity for themselves, their communities, and the globe.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Engineer, Software Development

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are looking for a full-time Quality Assurance Engineer to join our new Lagos, cross-functional software development teams, which will build the software that powers and improves our platform of educational tools.
	The Lagos team will have a special focus on the parts of the system that are more customer-facing, experimental, or strategically important.
	The key to the QA role on our teams is being a divergent thinker with a curious mind - a collaborator with developers in building great software, not an adversary, nor an order taker.
	If you’re looking to be a gatekeeper, NewGlobe will not be right for you. If you’re looking to get a perfect set of requirements handed to you, NewGlobe will not be right for you. On the other hand, if you’re all about being an enabler of teams, curiosity and innovation, and more than anything, about automation and repeatability, then NewGlobe might just be your next gig.
	You will report to one of our software development team leads.


What You Will Do


	Collaborate with team members and business stakeholders to define acceptance criteria for software development projects, including participation in the design and estimation of new software, to ensure that testing is not an afterthought
	Create and execute testing plans that include an appropriate combination of manual and automated testing, carefully reporting issues using structured tools and standards
	Advocate for a culture of automation and repeatability - and then get your hands dirty to make it a reality
	Follow testing practices to ensure the functional correctness, usability, scalability, and security of NewGlobe software
	Help resolve “works on my machine” automated testing issues by pairing with our software developers to run down environmental, network, or other complicating factors
	Deliver and manage automated testing tools for the constantly evolving needs of our various software platforms, including a range of web and mobile applications running across a variety of devices
	Integrate automated testing tools into build pipelines to promote a culture of continuous delivery
	Participate in the design, rollout, and use of monitoring tools in order to identify and resolve issues and ensure continuous integration of data into warehouses
	Devise approaches for validating and monitoring off-the-shelf and third-party software
	Participate in the design and use of application monitoring tools to reduce mean time to response and to maximise uptime
	Provide third-line technical support to in-house users


What You Should Have


	Bachelor's Degree with excellent academic performance
	4+ years of overall commercial software experience at the enterprise level
	Experience with different testing tools and patterns, such as contract testing for APIs and end-to-end testing for apps. Proficiency with other types of testing, such as load testing, penetration and security testing, or usability testing a major plus
	Comfort with both Windows and Linux/Ubuntu
	A desire to learn new tech stacks and frameworks - if you’ve only got experience in a particular platform and you think that makes it the right answer, NewGlobe is not right for you


Nice to have:


	DevOps experience at the enterprise level
	Hands-on experience with continuous delivery platforms (e.g. Jenkins, Go. CD, CircleCI, TFS)
	Hands-on experience with Code as Infrastructure tools (Puppet, Chef, Ansible, etc.)
	Experience in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure)
	Experience in containerisation (Docker) and containerisation infrastructure (Kubernetes, Docker Swarm, etc.)
	A proactive mindset and comfort working without constant supervision
	Flexibility to respond to the changing needs of the business
	Ability to prioritise and carry out multiple tasks
	Fast learner, energetic, and enthusiastic.

Senior Customer Service Representative Job at Timeless Telecoms




        
    

        
        Timeless Telecom was established in 2009 with the primary purpose of providing internet services. We started as an independent provider of last-mile, satellite-based, connectivity and access solutions for their mining and engineering clients in Nigerla. We are one of Nigeria's fastest-growing ISPs. We understand ICT technology, and we specalised in providing you with top-quality broadband, voice, as well as other related ICT equipment. What makes us unique is that we are an end-to-end provider, which means that when you deal with us, we own and manage your whole Telecoms and Broadband infrastructure.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Customer Service Representative

  "title": "Key Account Manager",
  "description": "Paga is on a mission to enable one billion Africans and millions of SMEs to pay, preserve hard-earnedincome, participate in global trade, and purchase goods and services at home and abroad. We have built amulticurrency, cloud-based payments and financial services engine that leverages multiple business servicesbuilt on the same best-in-class infrastructure to serve the ecosystem through three businesses - Paga Engine(Enterprise B2B - www.pagaengine.com); Paga (Consumer - www.Paga.com); and Doroki (SME Retail -www.doroki.com).

Paga has three licenses with the Central Bank of Nigeria &ndash; mobile money, internationalremittance, and a microfinance bank. Paga is headquartered out of the United Kingdom and has operatingentities in Nigeria, United States, and Ethiopia. Founded in 2009, Paga now serves millions of users andbusinesses across Africa and beyond.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Key Account Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;(Hybrid)
Sector: Financial Services

About the Role


	Reporting to the Partnerships Lead, the Key Account Manager will be responsible for managing relationships with key partners, to grow the Company&rsquo;s business and to ensure that clients&rsquo; needs are met.


Responsibilities and Tasks
Client/Stakeholder Management:


	Attracting and managing key account relationships to on-board new deal/projects and manage existing business for high performance; this includes serving as the primary Account Manager and point person for a portfolio of clients
	Get understanding of clients&rsquo; needs and requirements and proffer possible solutions
	Collaborating with the Partnerships Lead and other Account Managers to achieve team goals, and also representing the department as needed in line with Company policies
	Performing research, developing ideas and creating new business opportunities for the Company
	Designing and building reports to communicate ideas and status updates
	Organizing materials and information for tender submissions, including liaison with other units within the Company on submission of bid documents
	Providing inputs to management for developing capabilities in new areas of strategic value
	Serve as intermediary between the clients and the internal teams
	Communicating with cohorts in client and potential client organizations, professionally and socially (where appropriate), to maintain relationships with clients.


Minimum Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in a Business Development or Sales function and providing solutions to clients&rsquo; need
	Experience in technical payment products.&nbsp;


Skills &amp; Qualifications:
Job-specific Skills and Requirements:


	Exceptional attention to detail
	Resourcefulness and self-motivation, with the ability to perform with minimal supervision
	Well-organized, with ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects simultaneously
	Exceptional market/customer awareness and networking skills
	Strong proficiency in use of business email and Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
	Experienced in development of business processes, models, and plans, including basic financial analysis in Excel
	Excellent written and oral communication skills
	Adaptability, ability to work in a team environment
	Confidentiality and the highest ethical standards must be maintained in all activities
	The Key Account Manager will be required to provide assistance to the Company in a wide range of areas, including duties not directly related to his/her area of experience or expertise, in which case proper instruction will be provided by the Company

Key Account Manager Job at Paga Limited




        
    

        
        Paga is on a mission to enable one billion Africans and millions of SMEs to pay, preserve hard-earnedincome, participate in global trade, and purchase goods and services at home and abroad. We have built amulticurrency, cloud-based payments and financial services engine that leverages multiple business servicesbuilt on the same best-in-class infrastructure to serve the ecosystem through three businesses - Paga Engine(Enterprise B2B - www.pagaengine.com); Paga (Consumer - www.Paga.com); and Doroki (SME Retail -www.doroki.com).
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Paga has three licenses with the Central Bank of Nigeria – mobile money, internationalremittance, and a microfinance bank. Paga is headquartered out of the United Kingdom and has operatingentities in Nigeria, United States, and Ethiopia. Founded in 2009, Paga now serves millions of users andbusinesses across Africa and beyond.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Key Account Manager

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Sector: Financial Services

About the Role


	Reporting to the Partnerships Lead, the Key Account Manager will be responsible for managing relationships with key partners, to grow the Company’s business and to ensure that clients’ needs are met.


Responsibilities and Tasks
Client/Stakeholder Management:


	Attracting and managing key account relationships to on-board new deal/projects and manage existing business for high performance; this includes serving as the primary Account Manager and point person for a portfolio of clients
	Get understanding of clients’ needs and requirements and proffer possible solutions
	Collaborating with the Partnerships Lead and other Account Managers to achieve team goals, and also representing the department as needed in line with Company policies
	Performing research, developing ideas and creating new business opportunities for the Company
	Designing and building reports to communicate ideas and status updates
	Organizing materials and information for tender submissions, including liaison with other units within the Company on submission of bid documents
	Providing inputs to management for developing capabilities in new areas of strategic value
	Serve as intermediary between the clients and the internal teams
	Communicating with cohorts in client and potential client organizations, professionally and socially (where appropriate), to maintain relationships with clients.


Minimum Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in a Business Development or Sales function and providing solutions to clients’ need
	Experience in technical payment products. 


Skills & Qualifications:
Job-specific Skills and Requirements:


	Exceptional attention to detail
	Resourcefulness and self-motivation, with the ability to perform with minimal supervision
	Well-organized, with ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects simultaneously
	Exceptional market/customer awareness and networking skills
	Strong proficiency in use of business email and Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
	Experienced in development of business processes, models, and plans, including basic financial analysis in Excel
	Excellent written and oral communication skills
	Adaptability, ability to work in a team environment
	Confidentiality and the highest ethical standards must be maintained in all activities
	The Key Account Manager will be required to provide assistance to the Company in a wide range of areas, including duties not directly related to his/her area of experience or expertise, in which case proper instruction will be provided by the Company
